post_title: 5 Cybersecurity Best Practices for Your Nonprofit

images.alt: PageSpeed Insights report showing scores: 91 for Performance, 82 for Accessibility, 100 for Best Practices, and 90 for SEO.
images.section_heading: 1) Use a premium hosting service for your website.

images.alt: Diagram comparing Single Opt-in and Double Opt-in processes for email marketing, showing how users subscribe or fail to confirm subscription via email.
images.section_heading: 2) Require a double opt-in subscription process for your email list(s).

images.alt: Meta Accounts Center interface showing Password and Security settings, including options for changing password, two-factor authentication, and security checks.
images.section_heading: 3) Enable two-factor authentification for all social media accounts.

images.alt: 1Password homepage with the tagline 'Every sign-in, secure' displayed on a gradient blue and cream background, alongside navigation and call-to-action buttons.
Certificate in AI for Marketing & Fundraising awarded by Nonprofit Tech for Good, signed by CEO Heather Mansfield, with a blue award banner.
images.section_heading: 5) Update your software often and backup your data daily.
